title: Davallia blumei
taxonomy: Davallia blumei is a species of fern in the family Davalliaceae. The Davalliaceae family is a small group of epiphytic ferns, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Davallia blumei is native to Southeast Asia and is known to thrive in humid environments. This species of fern is often grown for its attractive fronds and can be found in various horticultural settings. It is classified as a species of Davallia, a genus of ferns that are typically epiphytic and have a wide range of habitats.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions.
